Q: Who developed the Paxos consensus algorithm?
A:
output:
Leslie Lamport.
Paxos is a family of algorithms that was originally designed by computer scientist and mathematician Leslie Lamport to solve a problem in fault-tolerant distributed computing systems. The core idea behind this set of protocols is that when multiple nodes have different information, there needs to be an agreed-upon method for selecting one piece of data as valid over others.

output:
HTTPS uses SSL/TLS (Secure Socket Layer/Transport Layer Security) in order to provide secure communication between a client and server. It's most commonly used with web browsers, but it can also be used for VoIP, IM or email. HTTPS encrypts data sent from the browser to the website using symmetric cryptography algorithms such as AES and RSA.
HTTPS stands for HyperText Transfer Protocol Secure and provides encryption of the communications between your browser and a website. This prevents hackers from intercepting information you send

Abstract syntax tree (AST) is a compact representation of this information. It can be used to store parse trees for later processing, and it serves as an intermediate language in many compilers.
The abstract syntax tree (AST), also called “parse tree”, is constructed by computer programming language parsers during analysis phase—the stage preceding synthesis phase when the program is executed. prompt:
Q: Which scientist is associated with the incompleteness theorems in mathematical logic?
A:
output:
Kurt Godel
The above Q&A is part of a series that we’re running to celebrate the 100th anniversary of Kurt Gödel’s birth.
Gödel was born on April 28, 1906 and died on January 14, 1978. He is best known for his incompleteness theorem, which shows that any consistent axiomatic system powerful enough to describe arithmetic includes undecidable propositions (true statements that cannot be proven from the given set of axioms).

prompt:
Q: What consensus protocol was designed as a more understandable alternative to Paxos?
A:
output:
Raft

Explanation:
Raft is a consensus algorithm that simplifies the complexity of Paxos by making it easier for developers and operators to understand. It provides strong consistency, high availability, and fault tolerance while avoiding common pitfalls in distributed systems.

Raft is an excellent choice when you need a reliable and easy-to-understand solution for achieving consensus among nodes in your network.

Source
https://www.section.io/engineering-education/raft-consensus-algorithm-introduction/

In 1965, Richard Dawkins was a graduate student in the Zoology Department at Oxford University. He was preparing his doctoral thesis on mimicry rings among bumblebees when he read an essay by John Maynard Smith called The Causes of Evolution (Maynard Smith’s essay later became Chapter One of his excellent book On Evolution.) This paper provided new insights into animal communication and opened up for Dawkins an entirely different way of thinking about evolution.
